attributes.description:
Controls the drop shadow effect around the calendar view. This creates a subtle elevation effect that makes the calendar appear to float above the background.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-view-shadow

Sets the border color of the calendar view. This determines what color the border around the calendar appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-view-border

Sets the border style of the calendar view. Acceptable values: solid, dashed, dotted, double, groove, ridge, inset, outset, none.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-view-border-style

Controls the border thickness of the calendar view. This determines how thick the border around the calendar appears.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-view-border-width

Controls the border radius for rounded corners of the calendar view. This makes the calendar appear with rounded corners instead of sharp edges.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-view-radius

Sets the background color of buttons in the calendar component. This determines what color appears behind the button content.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-button-bg-color

Sets the border color of buttons in the calendar component. This determines what color the border around buttons appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-button-border-color

Sets the vertical padding (top and bottom) of the calendar header. This creates spacing above and below the header content.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-header-vertical-padding

Sets the horizontal padding (left and right) of the calendar header. This creates spacing on the sides of the header content.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-header-horizontal-padding

Controls the thickness/boldness of the text in the calendar header. Acceptable values: normal, bold, 100-900.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-header-text-font-weight

Sets spacing between events in the calendar. This creates visual separation between different calendar events.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-events-gap

Sets the background color of calendar events. This determines what color appears behind event content.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-event-background

Sets the text color of calendar events. This determines what color the event text appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-event-color

Sets the background color for today's date in the calendar. This determines what color appears behind today's date.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-event-today-background

Sets the text color for today's date in the calendar. This determines what color today's date text appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-event-today-color

Sets the color of the dot indicator for today's date in the calendar. This determines what color the dot appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-event-today-dot

Sets the color of anchor links in the calendar component. This determines what color calendar navigation links appear in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-fc-anchor-color

Controls the width of the calendar component. This determines how wide the calendar appears on the screen.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-width

Controls the minimum height of the calendar component. This determines the minimum vertical space the calendar occupies.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-min-height

Controls the minimum width of the calendar component. This determines the minimum horizontal space the calendar occupies.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-min-width

Sets the background color of the calendar header. This is the background color that appears behind the header content.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-header-background-color

Controls the bottom border thickness of the calendar header. This determines how thick the border at the bottom of the header appears.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-header-border-bottom-width

Sets the border style of the calendar header. Acceptable values: solid, dashed, dotted.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-header-border-style

Controls the bottom border thickness of weekday labels in the calendar. This determines how thick the border at the bottom of weekday labels appears.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-weekday-border-bottom-width

Sets the border style of weekday labels in the calendar. Acceptable values: solid, dashed, dotted, double, groove, ridge, inset, outset, none.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-weekday-border-style

Sets the padding of weekday labels in the calendar. This creates spacing around the weekday text.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-weekday-padding

Sets the typeface (font style) for weekday text in the calendar. This determines whether the weekday text appears in Arial, Times New Roman, or other font styles.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-weekday-text-font-family

Controls the size of the weekday text in the calendar. This affects how large the weekday labels appear.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-weekday-text-font-size

Sets the border color of calendar day cells. This determines what color the border around each day appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-day-border-color

Sets the text color for days that are not in the current month. This determines what color these days appear in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-not-day-of-month-color

Controls the thickness/boldness of text for days that are not in the current month. Acceptable values: normal, bold, 100-900.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-not-day-of-month-font-weight

Sets the first color variant for event days in the calendar. This determines what color appears for the first event indicator.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-event-day1-color1

Sets the second color variant for event days in the calendar. This determines what color appears for the second event indicator.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-event-day1-color2

Sets the third color variant for event days in the calendar. This determines what color appears for the third event indicator.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-event-day1-color3

Sets the color of the previous month button in the calendar. This determines what color the navigation button appears in.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-prev-month-btn-color

Controls the width of the calendar header skeleton (loading state). This determines how wide the skeleton loader appears.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-header-skeleton-width

Controls the bottom margin of the calendar header skeleton. This creates spacing below the skeleton loader.
CSS variable: --wm-calendar-header-skeleton-margin-bottom
